title: Israeli Strikes Kill 5 Palestinians in Two Separate Gaza Attacks
detail: Israeli forces conducted at least two separate strikes in Gaza on March 31, killing at least five Palestinians, according to the Gaza Health Ministry. The attacks continued the pattern of IDF operations inside Gaza since the ceasefire took hold in October 2025, with the IDF citing ongoing Hamas ceasefire violations and armed activity as justification. The dead were transferred to Al-Nasser and Al-Ahli hospitals. Gaza's cumulative death toll since October 7, 2023 stands at 72,267+ documented, with 691+ killed since the October 2025 ceasefire.

sources.name: Gaza Ministry of Health — Daily casualty update, March 31, 2026

title: WSJ: Trump Willing to End Iran War Without Requiring Strait of Hormuz Reopening; Hegseth Calls for Diplomacy
detail: The Wall Street Journal reported on March 31 that President Trump told aides he is willing to end the US-Israel war on Iran without requiring the Strait of Hormuz to be formally reopened as a precondition for a ceasefire — a significant shift from the administration's stated negotiating position. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth separately called on Iran's 'new regime' to pursue a diplomatic settlement to the conflict. Secretary of State Rubio had told G7 foreign ministers earlier in the week that the Iran war would continue 'another two to four weeks.' The reported diplomatic flexibility came as Pakistan and China jointly released a five-point peace proposal that included securing maritime passage through the Strait of Hormuz, with both nations calling for an immediate ceasefire. IRGC generals simultaneously announced Iran would begin targeting US companies operating in the Middle East region starting April 1.

sources.name: Wall Street Journal — Trump willing to end Iran war without Hormuz reopening, March 31, 2026
Haaretz — Israel-Iran War Day 32 liveblog, March 31, 2026
Al Jazeera — Iran war diplomatic developments, March 31, 2026

title: France Denies Airspace for US Weapons to Israel; Italy Blocks Aircraft at Sicily Base — NATO Fracture Deepens
detail: France formally declined to allow Israel to use French airspace to transport US-supplied weapons for operations against Iran on March 31 — the first such refusal by a NATO ally since the war began February 28. Italy blocked several US military aircraft from landing at a key US air base in Sicily that has been used as a logistics hub for supporting Israeli and US operations against Iran. The dual refusals mark a significant escalation in transatlantic diplomatic friction over the Iran war and follow earlier NATO ally criticism of the US-Israel Iran campaign. France's refusal is particularly significant given it is a nuclear power and permanent UN Security Council member. European NATO members have broadly declined to join Trump's 'Board of Peace' and have increasingly distanced themselves from the Iran military operation.

sources.name: Haaretz — Day 32 liveblog: France denies airspace, Italy blocks US aircraft, March 31, 2026
Reuters — NATO allies balk at Iran war support, March 31, 2026
Al Jazeera — Western divisions over Iran war, March 31, 2026

title: CNN News Team Assaulted and Detained by Israeli Soldiers in West Bank
detail: An Israeli military unit assaulted and detained a CNN news team operating in the West Bank on March 31. The incident added to a mounting toll of press freedom violations documented by Reporters Without Borders (RSF) and the Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ), which had recorded over 220 journalists killed since the conflict began in October 2023. The CNN detention drew condemnation from the Committee to Protect Journalists and RSF; CNN said the crew was eventually released. The detention occurred during a period of elevated media-military tensions in the West Bank, following the killing of three journalists by Israeli strikes in Lebanon the previous day. Israel has been under sustained international criticism for its record on press freedom during the Gaza war.

sources.name: WSWS — CNN crew detained by Israeli soldiers in West Bank, March 31, 2026
CNN — Statement on crew detention, March 31, 2026
Committee to Protect Journalists — West Bank press freedom, March 2026
